arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try downloading the debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server using the BuildId
# BuildId: 4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801
BUILDID="4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801"

# Try ELF buildid format on Microsoft symbol server
echo "=== Trying Microsoft symbol server ==="
curl -sS -w "\nHTTP %{http_code}\n" -o /dev/null "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so"
curl -sS -w "\nHTTP %{http_code}\n" -o /dev/null "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"
curl -sS -w "\nHTTP %{http_code}\n" -o /dev/null "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so.dbg/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so.dbg"

# Also try NuGet symbol server
echo "=== Trying NuGet symbol server ==="
curl -sS -w "\nHTTP %{http_code}\n" -o /dev/null "https://symbols.nuget.org/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so"
curl -sS -w "\nHTTP %{http_code}\n" -o /dev/null "https://symbols.nuget.org/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"
arguments.description: Try downloading debug symbols from symbol servers

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Try downloading debug symbols from symbol servers

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames from this tombstone. The frames #01 through #06 are from `libcoreclr.so` with BuildId `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`.

To symbolicate these, I need the debug symbols for `libcoreclr.so`. The approach would be:

1. Use the BuildId to find the matching debug symbols from Microsoft's symbol server
2. Use a tool like `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line` to resolve the addresses


3. Microsoft publishes .NET runtime symbols on their symbol server at `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/`, where I can construct a URL using the BuildId to fetch the debug symbols for libcoreclr.so

. The exact format for ELF files varies—it could be the standard pattern with `elf-buildid-<buildid>` or a separate `.dbg` file with `elf-buildid-sym-<buildid>`. I should try using the `dotnet-symbol` tool, which can handle BuildId-based symbol downloads from the Microsoft symbol server. The BuildId might be from a synthetic example rather than a real published runtime, so let me try downloading the symbols from the Microsoft server and check what tools are available like llvm-symbolizer or addr2line. Given this is a .NET for Android app using CoreCLR, I should attempt to fetch the symbols and see what I can resolve.

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5

curl -sS -w "\nHTTP %{http_code}\n" -o /dev/null "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so"
curl -sS -w "\nHTTP %{http_code}\n" -o /dev/null "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"
curl -sS -w "\nHTTP %{http_code}\n" -o /dev/null "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so.dbg/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so.dbg"

# Also try NuGet symbol server

curl -sS -w "\nHTTP %{http_code}\n" -o /dev/null "https://symbols.nuget.org/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so"
